mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.1% represents an emergency zero-lower-bound regime. Sustained FEDFUNDS below 0.25% signals continued monetary accommodation, reducing equity discount rates and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any rolling 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 8.4% signals ongoing labor market slack from COVID-19 shock. A month-over-month decline in UNRATE of more than 0.3 percentage points indicates accelerating recovery momentum, triggering positive equity returns as growth expectations improve over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following UNRATE drops > 0.3pp are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.5%, t-test p > 0.10), or signal triggers in fewer than 15% of months.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) at 535,982 million dollars reflect consumer spending recovery from pandemic lows. A month-over-month increase in RSAFS exceeding 2% signals sustained demand momentum that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following RSAFS growth > 2% are not significantly positive (mean return < 1.5%, t-test p > 0.10), or signal fails to trigger in at least 20% of months.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 101.36 indicates manufacturing output near pre-pandemic levels. Sustained INDPRO growth above 100.5 combined with month-over-month increases exceeding 0.5% signals supply-side recovery that reduces inflation risk and supports equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns during periods of INDPRO > 100.5 with MoM growth > 0.5% are not significantly positive (mean < 0.3%, t-test p > 0.15), or condition triggers in fewer than 25% of months.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 1,496 thousand units and payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 140,914 thousand jointly signal labor market strength and residential investment recovery. When both series show month-over-month growth exceeding 1%, this interaction indicates broad-based demand momentum that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST MoM growth > 1% AND PAYEMS MoM growth > 1%
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns when both HOUST and PAYEMS show MoM growth > 1% are not significantly positive (mean < 0.2%, t-test p > 0.20), or joint condition triggers in fewer than 10% of months.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) at 259.68 combined with FEDFUNDS at 0.1% creates a deeply negative real interest rate environment. When CPI year-over-year growth exceeds 1.5% while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, this interaction signals financial repression that compresses real yields and drives equity valuations higher over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns during periods of CPI YoY > 1.5% and FEDFUNDS < 0.5% are not significantly positive (mean < 1%, t-test p > 0.15), or real rate compression fails to predict returns in at least 30% of qualifying periods.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 140,914 thousand reflects ongoing pandemic-driven job losses relative to pre-COVID levels. In the post-shock recovery regime, when PAYEMS month-over-month growth exceeds 0.8%, this signals accelerating labor market healing that supports consumer confidence and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days due to mean reversion from elevated valuations.
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with elevated equity valuations
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns following PAYEMS MoM growth > 0.8% are positive in more than 60% of instances (contradicting negative direction), or signal triggers in fewer than 15% of months.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) at 17,282.2 billion chained 2017 dollars remains below pre-pandemic levels. In the recovery regime, when GDPC1 growth accelerates to exceed 2% annualized quarter-over-quarter while RSAFS simultaneously shows month-over-month growth above 1.5%, this interaction signals sustained demand recovery that produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth > 2% AND RSAFS MoM growth > 1.5%
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns when both GDPC1 QoQ growth > 2% and RSAFS MoM growth > 1.5% are not significantly positive (mean < 1.2%, t-test p > 0.12), or joint condition triggers in fewer than 8% of quarters.
